chapter: Chapter: Electricity – Magnetic and Heating Effects

questions.question: What is electric current?
questions.answer: Electric current is the flow of electric charges through a conductor.

questions.question: What is an electric circuit?
questions.answer: An electric circuit is a closed path through which electric current flows.

questions.question: Name a device used to produce electricity in a circuit.
questions.answer: A cell or battery is used to produce electricity in a circuit.

questions.question: What is a conductor?
questions.answer: A conductor is a material that allows electric current to pass through it easily.

questions.question: Give one example of a conductor.
questions.answer: Copper is an example of a conductor.

questions.question: What is an insulator?
questions.answer: An insulator is a material that does not allow electric current to pass through it easily.

questions.question: Give one example of an insulator.
questions.answer: Rubber is an example of an insulator.

questions.question: What is the function of a switch in a circuit?
questions.answer: A switch is used to open or close an electric circuit.

questions.question: What happens when a circuit is open?
questions.answer: When a circuit is open, electric current cannot flow.

questions.question: What happens when a circuit is closed?
questions.answer: When a circuit is closed, electric current flows through it.

questions.question: What is the heating effect of electric current?
questions.answer: The heating effect of electric current is the production of heat when electric current flows through a conductor.

questions.question: Name one device that works using the heating effect of current.
questions.answer: An electric iron works using the heating effect of current.

questions.question: What is a fuse?
questions.answer: A fuse is a safety device that melts and breaks the circuit when excessive current flows.

questions.question: Why is a fuse used in electric circuits?
questions.answer: A fuse protects electrical appliances from damage due to excessive current.

questions.question: What is an electromagnet?
questions.answer: An electromagnet is a magnet produced by passing electric current through a coil of wire.

questions.question: Name one material used as the core of an electromagnet.
questions.answer: Soft iron is used as the core of an electromagnet.

questions.question: What happens to a compass near a current-carrying wire?
questions.answer: The compass needle deflects due to the magnetic effect of electric current.

questions.question: Name one device that uses an electromagnet.
questions.answer: An electric bell uses an electromagnet.

questions.question: What is the function of connecting wires in a circuit?
questions.answer: Connecting wires provide a path for electric current to flow.

questions.question: Why are wires usually covered with plastic or rubber?
questions.answer: Plastic or rubber acts as insulation and prevents electric shocks.

questions.question: What happens when too much current flows through a wire?
questions.answer: The wire may heat up and melt due to the heating effect.

questions.question: What is a battery?
questions.answer: A battery is a combination of two or more electric cells connected together.

questions.question: What is the role of an electric bulb in a circuit?
questions.answer: An electric bulb produces light when electric current flows through it.

questions.question: Name one appliance that uses electricity to produce heat.
questions.answer: An electric heater uses electricity to produce heat.

questions.question: Why is electricity important in daily life?
questions.answer: Electricity is important because it powers many devices and appliances used in daily life.
